source: Efficient Neural Architecture Search via Parameter Sharing.pdf

concepts.finding: ENAS reduces NAS cost (1000x less than original NAS) by forcing all child models to share weights, discovering PTB architecture with 55.8 perplexity and CIFAR-10 architecture with 2.89% error.
concepts.implication: Weight sharing across model candidates dramatically reduces search cost, making architecture search practical for production teams.
concepts.open_question: Can weight-sharing NAS scale to search ranking architectures with billions of parameters?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A weight-sharing NAS approach will discover a ranking model architecture matching hand-tuned production quality with 100x fewer GPU-hours of search.
